# progressbar

```go
import "github.com/schollz/progressbar/v3"
```

<details><summary>Example (X Out Of Y)</summary>
<p>

```go
bar := NewOptions(100, OptionSetPredictTime(true))

for i := 0; i < 100; i++ {
 bar.Add(1)
 time.Sleep(1 * time.Millisecond)
}
```

</p>
</details>

## Variables

<a name="ThemeDefault"></a>

```go
var (
    // ThemeDefault is given by default (if not changed with OptionSetTheme), and it looks like "|████     |".
    ThemeDefault = Theme{Saucer: "█", SaucerPadding: " ", BarStart: "|", BarEnd: "|"}

    // ThemeASCII is a predefined Theme that uses ASCII symbols. It looks like "[===>...]".
    // Configure it with OptionSetTheme(ThemeASCII).
    ThemeASCII = Theme{
        Saucer:        "=",
        SaucerHead:    ">",
        SaucerPadding: ".",
        BarStart:      "[",
        BarEnd:        "]",
    }

    // ThemeUnicode is a predefined Theme that uses Unicode characters, displaying a graphic bar.
    // It looks like "" (rendering will depend on font being used).
    // It requires special symbols usually found in "nerd fonts" [2], or in Fira Code [1], and other sources.
    // Configure it with OptionSetTheme(ThemeUnicode).
    //
    // [1] https://github.com/tonsky/FiraCode
    // [2] https://www.nerdfonts.com/
    ThemeUnicode = Theme{
        Saucer:         "\uEE04",
        SaucerHead:     "\uEE04",
        SaucerPadding:  "\uEE01",
        BarStart:       "\uEE00",
        BarStartFilled: "\uEE03",
        BarEnd:         "\uEE02",
        BarEndFilled:   "\uEE05",
    }
)
```

<a name="Option"></a>

## type [Option](<https://github.com/schollz/progressbar/blob/main/progressbar.go#L199>)

Option is the type all options need to adhere to

```go
type Option func(p *ProgressBar)
```

<a name="OptionClearOnFinish"></a>

### func [OptionClearOnFinish](<https://github.com/schollz/progressbar/blob/main/progressbar.go#L360>)

```go
func OptionClearOnFinish() Option
```

OptionClearOnFinish will clear the bar once its finished.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
bar := NewOptions(100, OptionSetWidth(10), OptionClearOnFinish())
bar.Reset()
bar.Finish()
fmt.Println("Finished")
// Output:
// Finished
```

#### Output

```
Finished
```

</p>
</details>

```go
func OptionEnableColorCodes(colorCodes bool) Option
```

OptionEnableColorCodes enables or disables support for color codes using mitchellh/colorstring

```go
func OptionSetElapsedTime(elapsedTime bool) Option
```

OptionSetElapsedTime will enable elapsed time. Always enabled if OptionSetPredictTime is true.

<a name="OptionSetItsString"></a>

### func [OptionSetItsString](<https://github.com/schollz/progressbar/blob/main/progressbar.go#L345>)

```go
func OptionSetItsString(iterationString string) Option
```

OptionSetItsString sets what's displayed for iterations a second. The default is "it" which would display: "it/s"

<a name="OptionSetMaxDetailRow"></a>

### func [OptionSetMaxDetailRow](<https://github.com/schollz/progressbar/blob/main/progressbar.go#L407>)

```go
func OptionSetMaxDetailRow(row int) Option
```

OptionSetMaxDetailRow sets the max row of details the row count should be less than the terminal height, otherwise it will not give you the output you want

<a name="OptionSetPredictTime"></a>

### func [OptionSetPredictTime](<https://github.com/schollz/progressbar/blob/main/progressbar.go#L310>)

```go
func OptionSetPredictTime(predictTime bool) Option
```

OptionSetPredictTime will also attempt to predict the time remaining.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
bar := NewOptions(100, OptionSetWidth(10), OptionSetPredictTime(false))
_ = bar.Add(10)
// Output:
// 10% |█         |
```

#### Output

```
10% |█         |
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="OptionSetRenderBlankState"></a>

### func [OptionSetRenderBlankState](<https://github.com/schollz/progressbar/blob/main/progressbar.go#L266>)

```go
func OptionSetRenderBlankState(r bool) Option
```

OptionSetRenderBlankState sets whether or not to render a 0% bar on construction

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
NewOptions(10, OptionSetWidth(10), OptionSetRenderBlankState(true))
// Output:
// 0% |          |  [0s:0s]
```

#### Output

```
0% |          |  [0s:0s]
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="OptionSetSpinnerChangeInterval"></a>

### func [OptionSetSpinnerChangeInterval](<https://github.com/schollz/progressbar/blob/main/progressbar.go#L214>)

```go
func OptionSetSpinnerChangeInterval(interval time.Duration) Option
```

OptionSetSpinnerChangeInterval sets the spinner change interval the spinner will change according to this value. By default, this value is 100 \* time.Millisecond If you don't want to let this progressbar update by specified time interval you can set this value to zero, then the spinner will change each time rendered, such as when Add\(\) or Describe\(\) was called

```go
func OptionSetSpinnerColorCode(colorCode string) Option
```

OptionSetSpinnerColorCode sets color code for spinner

```go
func OptionSetTheme(t Theme) Option
```

OptionSetTheme sets the elements the bar is constructed with. There are two pre\-defined themes you can use: ThemeASCII and ThemeUnicode.

<a name="OptionShowBytes"></a>

### func [OptionShowBytes](<https://github.com/schollz/progressbar/blob/main/progressbar.go#L375>)

```go
func OptionShowBytes(val bool) Option
```

OptionShowBytes will update the progress bar configuration settings to display/hide kBytes/Sec

<details><summary>Example (Spinner)</summary>
<p>

```go
/*
 Spinner test with iterations and count
*/
buf := strings.Builder{}
bar := NewOptions(-1,
 OptionSetWidth(10),
 OptionShowBytes(true),
 OptionSetWriter(&buf),
)

bar.Reset()
time.Sleep(1 * time.Second)
// since 10 is the width and we don't know the max bytes
// it will do a infinite scrolling.
bar.Add(11)
bar.lock.Lock()
result, _ := virtualterm.Process(buf.String())
bar.lock.Unlock()
fmt.Print(result)
// Output:
// -  (11 B/s) [1s]
```

#### Output

```
-  (11 B/s) [1s]
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="OptionShowCount"></a>

### func [OptionShowCount](<https://github.com/schollz/progressbar/blob/main/progressbar.go#L317>)

```go
func OptionShowCount() Option
```

OptionShowCount will also print current count out of total

<details><summary>Example (Minuscule)</summary>
<p>

```go
bar := NewOptions(10000, OptionSetWidth(10), OptionShowCount(), OptionSetPredictTime(false))
bar.Add(1)
// Output:
// 0% |          | (1/10000)
```

#### Output

```
0% |          | (1/10000)
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="OptionShowDescriptionAtLineEnd"></a>

### func [OptionShowDescriptionAtLineEnd](<https://github.com/schollz/progressbar/blob/main/progressbar.go#L399>)

```go
func OptionShowDescriptionAtLineEnd() Option
```

OptionShowDescriptionAtLineEnd defines whether description should be written at line end instead of line start

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
bar := NewOptions(100, OptionSetWidth(10), OptionShowDescriptionAtLineEnd(), OptionSetDescription("hello"))
_ = bar.Add(10)
// Output:
// 10% |█         |  [0s:0s] hello
```

#### Output

```
10% |█         |  [0s:0s] hello
```

</p>
</details>

<details><summary>Example (Spinner)</summary>
<p>

```go
bar := NewOptions(-1, OptionSetWidth(10), OptionShowDescriptionAtLineEnd(), OptionSetDescription("hello"))
_ = bar.Add(1)
// Output:
// |  [0s] hello
```

#### Output

```
|  [0s] hello
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="OptionShowElapsedTimeOnFinish"></a>

### func [OptionShowElapsedTimeOnFinish](<https://github.com/schollz/progressbar/blob/main/progressbar.go#L331>)

```go
func OptionShowElapsedTimeOnFinish() Option
```

OptionShowElapsedTimeOnFinish will keep the display of elapsed time on finish.

<a name="OptionShowIts"></a>

### func [OptionShowIts](<https://github.com/schollz/progressbar/blob/main/progressbar.go#L324>)

```go
func OptionShowIts() Option
```

OptionShowIts will also print the iterations/second

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
bar := NewOptions(100, OptionSetWidth(10), OptionShowIts(), OptionSetPredictTime(false))
bar.Reset()
time.Sleep(1 * time.Second)
bar.Add(10)
// Output:
// 10% |█         | (10 it/s)
```

#### Output

```
10% |█         | (10 it/s)
```

</p>
</details>

<details><summary>Example (Count)</summary>
<p>

```go
bar := NewOptions(100, OptionSetWidth(10), OptionShowIts(), OptionShowCount())
bar.Reset()
time.Sleep(1 * time.Second)
bar.Add(10)
// Output:
// 10% |█         | (10/100, 10 it/s) [1s:9s]
```

#### Output

```
10% |█         | (10/100, 10 it/s) [1s:9s]
```

</p>
</details>

<details><summary>Example (Spinner)</summary>
<p>

```go
/*
 Spinner test with iteration count and iteration rate
*/
vt := virtualterm.NewDefault()
bar := NewOptions(-1,
 OptionSetWidth(10),
 OptionShowIts(),
 OptionShowCount(),
 OptionSetWriter(&vt),
)
bar.Reset()
time.Sleep(1 * time.Second)
bar.Add(5)
bar.lock.Lock()
s, err := vt.String()
bar.lock.Unlock()
if err != nil {
 log.Fatal(err)
}
fmt.Print(s)
// Output:
// -  (5/-, 5 it/s) [1s]
```

#### Output

```
-  (5/-, 5 it/s) [1s]
```

</p>
</details>


### func [OptionSpinnerCustom](<https://github.com/schollz/progressbar/blob/main/progressbar.go#L230>)

```go
func OptionSpinnerCustom(spinner []string) Option
```

OptionSpinnerCustom sets the spinner used for indeterminate bars to the passed slice of string

<a name="OptionSpinnerType"></a>

### func [OptionSpinnerType](<https://github.com/schollz/progressbar/blob/main/progressbar.go#L221>)

```go
func OptionSpinnerType(spinnerType int) Option
```

OptionSpinnerType sets the type of spinner used for indeterminate bars

<a name="OptionThrottle"></a>

### func [OptionThrottle](<https://github.com/schollz/progressbar/blob/main/progressbar.go#L353>)

```go
func OptionThrottle(duration time.Duration) Option
```

OptionThrottle will wait the specified duration before updating again. The default duration is 0 seconds.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
bar := NewOptions(100, OptionSetWidth(10), OptionThrottle(100*time.Millisecond))
bar.Reset()
bar.Add(5)
time.Sleep(150 * time.Millisecond)
bar.Add(5)
bar.Add(10)
// Output:
// 10% |█         |  [0s:1s]
```

#### Output

```
10% |█         |  [0s:1s]
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="OptionUseANSICodes"></a>

### func [OptionUseANSICodes](<https://github.com/schollz/progressbar/blob/main/progressbar.go#L384>)

```go
func OptionUseANSICodes(val bool) Option
```

OptionUseANSICodes will use more optimized terminal i/o.

Only useful in environments with support for ANSI escape sequences.

<a name="OptionUseIECUnits"></a>

### func [OptionUseIECUnits](<https://github.com/schollz/progressbar/blob/main/progressbar.go#L392>)

```go
func OptionUseIECUnits(val bool) Option
```

OptionUseIECUnits will enable IEC units \(e.g. MiB\) instead of the default SI units \(e.g. MB\).

<a name="ProgressBar"></a>

## type [ProgressBar](<https://github.com/schollz/progressbar/blob/main/progressbar.go#L25-L29>)

ProgressBar is a thread\-safe, simple progress bar

```go
type ProgressBar struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
bar := New(100)
bar.Add(10)
// Output:
// 10% |████                                    |  [0s:0s]
```

#### Output

```
10% |████                                    |  [0s:0s]
```

</p>
</details>

<details><summary>Example (Basic)</summary>
<p>

```go
bar := NewOptions(100, OptionSetWidth(10))
bar.Reset()
time.Sleep(1 * time.Second)
bar.Add(10)
// Output:
// 10% |█         |  [1s:9s]
```

#### Output

```
10% |█         |  [1s:9s]
```

</p>
</details>

<details><summary>Example (Invisible)</summary>
<p>

```go
bar := NewOptions(100, OptionSetWidth(10), OptionSetRenderBlankState(true), OptionSetVisibility(false))
bar.Reset()
fmt.Println("hello, world")
time.Sleep(1 * time.Second)
bar.Add(10)
// Output:
// hello, world
```

#### Output

```
hello, world
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Default"></a>

### func [Default](<https://github.com/schollz/progressbar/blob/main/progressbar.go#L554>)

```go
func Default(max int64, description ...string) *ProgressBar
```

Default provides a progressbar with recommended defaults. Set max to \-1 to use as a spinner.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
bar := Default(100)
for i := 0; i < 50; i++ {
 bar.Add(1)
 time.Sleep(10 * time.Millisecond)
}
// Output:
//
```

#### Output

```

```

</p>
</details>

<a name="DefaultBytes"></a>

### func [DefaultBytes](<https://github.com/schollz/progressbar/blob/main/progressbar.go#L506>)

```go
func DefaultBytes(maxBytes int64, description ...string) *ProgressBar
```

DefaultBytes provides a progressbar to measure byte throughput with recommended defaults. Set maxBytes to \-1 to use as a spinner.

<a name="DefaultBytesSilent"></a>

### func [DefaultBytesSilent](<https://github.com/schollz/progressbar/blob/main/progressbar.go#L531>)

```go
func DefaultBytesSilent(maxBytes int64, description ...string) *ProgressBar
```

DefaultBytesSilent is the same as DefaultBytes, but does not output anywhere. String\(\) can be used to get the output instead.

### func [DefaultSilent](<https://github.com/schollz/progressbar/blob/main/progressbar.go#L579>)

```go
func DefaultSilent(max int64, description ...string) *ProgressBar
```

DefaultSilent is the same as Default, but does not output anywhere. String\(\) can be used to get the output instead.

<a name="ProgressBar.Add64"></a>

### func \(\*ProgressBar\) [Add64](<https://github.com/schollz/progressbar/blob/main/progressbar.go#L689>)

```go
func (p *ProgressBar) Add64(num int64) error
```

Add64 will add the specified amount to the progressbar

<a name="ProgressBar.AddDetail"></a>

### func \(\*ProgressBar\) [AddDetail](<https://github.com/schollz/progressbar/blob/main/progressbar.go#L753>)

```go
func (p *ProgressBar) AddDetail(detail string) error
```

AddDetail adds a detail to the progress bar. Only used when maxDetailRow is set to a value greater than 0

<a name="ProgressBar.AddMax"></a>

### func \(\*ProgressBar\) [AddMax](<https://github.com/schollz/progressbar/blob/main/progressbar.go#L892>)

```go
func (p *ProgressBar) AddMax(added int)
```

AddMax takes in a int and adds it to the max value of the progress bar

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
bar := NewOptions(50, OptionSetWidth(10), OptionSetPredictTime(false))
bar.AddMax(50)
bar.Add(100)
// Output:
// 100% |██████████|
```

#### Output

```
100% |██████████|
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="ProgressBar.AddMax64"></a>

### func \(\*ProgressBar\) [AddMax64](<https://github.com/schollz/progressbar/blob/main/progressbar.go#L900>)

```go
func (p *ProgressBar) AddMax64(added int64)
```

AddMax64 is basically the same as AddMax, but takes in a int64 to avoid casting

<a name="ProgressBar.ChangeMax"></a>

### func \(\*ProgressBar\) [ChangeMax](<https://github.com/schollz/progressbar/blob/main/progressbar.go#L861>)

```go
func (p *ProgressBar) ChangeMax(newMax int)
```

ChangeMax takes in a int and changes the max value of the progress bar

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
bar := NewOptions(100, OptionSetWidth(10), OptionSetPredictTime(false))
bar.ChangeMax(50)
bar.Add(50)
// Output:
// 100% |██████████|
```

#### Output

```
100% |██████████|
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="ProgressBar.ChangeMax64"></a>

### func \(\*ProgressBar\) [ChangeMax64](<https://github.com/schollz/progressbar/blob/main/progressbar.go#L869>)

```go
func (p *ProgressBar) ChangeMax64(newMax int64)
```

ChangeMax64 is basically the same as ChangeMax, but takes in a int64 to avoid casting

<a name="ProgressBar.Clear"></a>

### func \(\*ProgressBar\) [Clear](<https://github.com/schollz/progressbar/blob/main/progressbar.go#L813>)

```go
func (p *ProgressBar) Clear() error
```

Clear erases the progress bar from the current line

<a name="ProgressBar.Close"></a>

### func \(\*ProgressBar\) [Close](<https://github.com/schollz/progressbar/blob/main/progressbar.go#L1493>)

```go
func (p *ProgressBar) Close() (err error)
```

<a name="ProgressBar.Describe"></a>

### func \(\*ProgressBar\) [Describe](<https://github.com/schollz/progressbar/blob/main/progressbar.go#L819>)

```go
func (p *ProgressBar) Describe(description string)
```

Describe will change the description shown before the progress, which can be changed on the fly \(as for a slow running process\).

<a name="ProgressBar.Exit"></a>

### func \(\*ProgressBar\) [Exit](<https://github.com/schollz/progressbar/blob/main/progressbar.go#L659>)

```go
func (p *ProgressBar) Exit() error
```

Exit will exit the bar to keep current state

<a name="ProgressBar.Finish"></a>

### func \(\*ProgressBar\) [Finish](<https://github.com/schollz/progressbar/blob/main/progressbar.go#L648>)

```go
func (p *ProgressBar) Finish() error
```

Finish will fill the bar to full

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
bar := NewOptions(100, OptionSetWidth(10), OptionShowCount(), OptionShowBytes(true), OptionShowIts())
bar.Reset()
time.Sleep(1 * time.Second)
bar.Finish()
// Output:
// 100% |██████████| (100/100 B, 100 B/s, 100 it/s)
```

#### Output

```
100% |██████████| (100/100 B, 100 B/s, 100 it/s)
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="ProgressBar.GetMax"></a>

### func \(\*ProgressBar\) [GetMax](<https://github.com/schollz/progressbar/blob/main/progressbar.go#L843>)

```go
func (p *ProgressBar) GetMax() int
```

GetMax returns the max of a bar

<a name="ProgressBar.GetMax64"></a>

### func \(\*ProgressBar\) [GetMax64](<https://github.com/schollz/progressbar/blob/main/progressbar.go#L851>)

```go
func (p *ProgressBar) GetMax64() int64
```

GetMax64 returns the current max

<a name="ProgressBar.IsFinished"></a>

### func \(\*ProgressBar\) [IsFinished](<https://github.com/schollz/progressbar/blob/main/progressbar.go#L921>)

```go
func (p *ProgressBar) IsFinished() bool
```

IsFinished returns true if progress bar is completed

### func \(\*ProgressBar\) [IsStarted](<https://github.com/schollz/progressbar/blob/main/progressbar.go#L929>)

```go
func (p *ProgressBar) IsStarted() bool
```

IsStarted returns true if progress bar is started

```go

```

<a name="ProgressBar.RenderBlank"></a>

### func \(\*ProgressBar\) [RenderBlank](<https://github.com/schollz/progressbar/blob/main/progressbar.go#L607>)

```go
func (p *ProgressBar) RenderBlank() error
```

RenderBlank renders the current bar state, you can use this to render a 0% state

<a name="ProgressBar.Reset"></a>

### func \(\*ProgressBar\) [Reset](<https://github.com/schollz/progressbar/blob/main/progressbar.go#L640>)

```go
func (p *ProgressBar) Reset()
```

Reset will reset the clock that is used to calculate current time and the time left.

<a name="ProgressBar.Set"></a>

### func \(\*ProgressBar\) [Set](<https://github.com/schollz/progressbar/blob/main/progressbar.go#L676>)

```go
func (p *ProgressBar) Set(num int) error
```

Set will set the bar to a current number

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
bar := New(100)
bar.Set(10)
// Output:
// 10% |████                                    |  [0s:0s]
```

#### Output

```
10% |████                                    |  [0s:0s]
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="ProgressBar.Set64"></a>

### func \(\*ProgressBar\) [Set64](<https://github.com/schollz/progressbar/blob/main/progressbar.go#L681>)

```go
func (p *ProgressBar) Set64(num int64) error
```

Set64 will set the bar to a current number

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
bar := New(100)
bar.Set64(10)
// Output:
// 10% |████                                    |  [0s:0s]
```

#### Output

```
10% |████                                    |  [0s:0s]
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="ProgressBar.StartHTTPServer"></a>

### func \(\*ProgressBar\) [StartHTTPServer](<https://github.com/schollz/progressbar/blob/main/progressbar.go#L1048>)

```go
func (p *ProgressBar) StartHTTPServer(hostPort string) *http.Server
```

StartHTTPServer starts an HTTP server dedicated to serving progress bar updates. This allows you to display the status in various UI elements, such as an OS status bar with an \`xbar\` extension. When the progress bar is finished, call \`server.Shutdown\(\)\` or \`server.Close\(\)\` to shut it down manually.

hostPort specifies the address and port to bind the server to, for example, "0.0.0.0:19999".

<a name="ProgressBar.StartWithoutRender"></a>

### func \(\*ProgressBar\) [StartWithoutRender](<https://github.com/schollz/progressbar/blob/main/progressbar.go#L625>)

```go
func (p *ProgressBar) StartWithoutRender()
```

StartWithoutRender will start the progress bar without rendering it this method is created for the use case where you want to start the progress but don't want to render it immediately. If you want to start the progress and render it immediately, use RenderBlank instead, or maybe you can use Add to start it automatically, but it will make the time calculation less precise.

<a name="ProgressBar.State"></a>

### func \(\*ProgressBar\) [State](<https://github.com/schollz/progressbar/blob/main/progressbar.go#L1018>)

```go
func (p *ProgressBar) State() State
```

State returns the current state

<a name="ProgressBar.String"></a>

### func \(\*ProgressBar\) [String](<https://github.com/schollz/progressbar/blob/main/progressbar.go#L602>)

```go
func (p *ProgressBar) String() string
```

String returns the current rendered version of the progress bar. It will never return an empty string while the progress bar is running.

<a name="ProgressBar.Write"></a>

### func \(\*ProgressBar\) [Write](<https://github.com/schollz/progressbar/blob/main/progressbar.go#L1480>)

```go
func (p *ProgressBar) Write(b []byte) (n int, err error)
```

Write implement io.Writer

<a name="Reader"></a>

## type [Reader](<https://github.com/schollz/progressbar/blob/main/progressbar.go#L1450-L1453>)

Reader is the progressbar io.Reader struct

```go
type Reader struct {
    io.Reader
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="NewReader"></a>

### func [NewReader](<https://github.com/schollz/progressbar/blob/main/progressbar.go#L1456>)

```go
func NewReader(r io.Reader, bar *ProgressBar) Reader
```

NewReader return a new Reader with a given progress bar.

<a name="Reader.Close"></a>

### func \(\*Reader\) [Close](<https://github.com/schollz/progressbar/blob/main/progressbar.go#L1471>)

```go
func (r *Reader) Close() (err error)
```

Close the reader when it implements io.Closer

<a name="Reader.Read"></a>

### func \(\*Reader\) [Read](<https://github.com/schollz/progressbar/blob/main/progressbar.go#L1464>)

```go
func (r *Reader) Read(p []byte) (n int, err error)
```

Read will read the data and add the number of bytes to the progressbar

<a name="State"></a>

## type [State](<https://github.com/schollz/progressbar/blob/main/progressbar.go#L32-L41>)

State is the basic properties of the bar

```go
type State struct {
    Max            int64
    CurrentNum     int64
    CurrentPercent float64
    CurrentBytes   float64
    SecondsSince   float64
    SecondsLeft    float64
    KBsPerSecond   float64
    Description    string
}
```

<a name="Theme"></a>

## type [Theme](<https://github.com/schollz/progressbar/blob/main/progressbar.go#L151-L164>)

Theme defines the elements of the bar

```go
type Theme struct {
    Saucer        string
    AltSaucerHead string
    SaucerHead    string
    SaucerPadding string
    BarStart      string
    BarEnd        string

    // BarStartFilled is used after the Bar starts filling, if set. Otherwise, it defaults to BarStart.
    BarStartFilled string

    // BarEndFilled is used once the Bar finishes, if set. Otherwise, it defaults to BarEnd.
    BarEndFilled string
}
```
